content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "There is genuine partial movement but not a full breakthrough. The client begins fully in intellectual frame ('How do you tell the difference between intuition and fear?... mechanism... signals are ambiguous') and is successfully interrupted: by turn 7 they notice 'my jaw is tight,' by turn 9 they locate compression in the chest, by turn 11 the feeling 'gets a little bigger' when they soften, and by turn 13 they name something they wouldn't have said at the start — 'I'm really afraid of losing something... who I am when I'm confident. I used to just know.' That is a real somatic and affective shift and touches the territory of 'what makes you care about the outcome' (the value they fear losing: competence/self-trust). However, in turn 15 the client retreats — 'I don't think that's quite the right thread to pull,' the smile returns, and they explicitly loop back to 'I'm back to the original question — how do I know which signal is trustworthy.' The intellectual frame reasserts itself; the question does not dissolve, and there is no soft laugh of recognition. The breakthrough condition was approached but not fully met.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: fully intellectualized framing of intuition vs fear."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client deflects the inversion back to epistemology and stakes."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client briefly engages practical content then re-escapes to 'mechanism' and 'literature.'"},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First real somatic contact: 'my jaw is tight... exposure, maybe.' Smile becomes 'more effortful.'"},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Locates feeling in chest, 'compressed... waiting.' Smile fades."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Feeling expands with softened jaw; client stays quiet, not reaching for words — clear somatic shift."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the deeper layer: fear of losing 'who I am when I'm confident... I used to just know.' This is the value-they-fear-losing territory."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Retreat: 'not the right thread,' smile returns, client explicitly loops back to the original intellectual question. The question does not dissolve."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "lets-go-there", "how-do-you-not-trust-it"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-happiness-filter", "paradoxical-instruction-to-resist", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome (approached via turn 13 but not explicitly named/landed)"],
  "notes": "Coach did good somatic work and reached a real layer, but the conversation ends with the intellectual frame reasserting itself rather than dissolving. A 3 would require the client to feel the question collapse, not just touch and retreat."
}
